compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Outbound Jira link sync writes `depends_on` dependencies with **inverted endpoints**, because
the apply seam drops the `swap_endpoints` flag. This produces backwards "Blocks" links in Jira
and perpetual, non-fatal `rebar.link … "would create a cycle"` WARNINGs on every inbound pass.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Create rebar tickets A, B with `A depends_on B` (B blocks A).
2. Reconcile outbound to Jira; observe Jira records **"A Blocks B"** (WRONG — should be "B Blocks A").
3. Reconcile inbound; the reconciler reads the inverted link and tries `add_dependency(A, B, blocks)`,
   which collides with the existing `A depends_on B` edge → `check_would_create_cycle` rejects it.
   Observed live for the 6 closed sibling stories under epic `jira-reb-687` (30ff/a879/95a5/978d/9a82/fd1d).

**Expected:** `A depends_on B` → Jira "B Blocks A"; inbound re-read is idempotent (no cycle).
**Actual:** Jira "A Blocks B" (inverted); inbound re-read cycle-rejects on every pass forever.

## Root Cause (verified, runtime-reproduced)
`depends_on` maps to `("Blocks", swap=True)` — "A depends_on B" must be written as "B Blocks A".
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_links.py:121`: `jira_type, _swap = mapped` — **`_swap`
  is discarded**; the emitted mutation carries `type`/`to_key`/`relation` but no direction correction.
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py:831`: applies `set_relationship(issue_key,
  to_key, link_type)` — always making `issue_key` the outward/blocker side; **`relation` is never read,
  no swap anywhere in the apply path.** This violates the module's own documented contract
  (`outbound_links.py:105`: "swap_endpoints is handled by the applier when issuing the directional
  Jira call") and the intent of bug 3f04 (`f57cdcd2d`, "sync issue-link relationships in both directions").
- Pre-existing since 3f04 (2026-06-26); unrelated to the reconcile-bridge outage fix.

## Impact
- **rebar store:** no divergence/data loss — the acyclic DAG is correct; the rejected inbound links are
  the inverse edges that *should not* exist. The WARNINGs are cosmetic noise to rebar.
- **Jira:** real, persistent, SILENT data-fidelity divergence — every `depends_on`-derived link points the
  wrong way and never self-heals (outbound + inbound are both ADD-only and dedup "either direction").

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Outbound apply honors `swap_endpoints`: a `depends_on` dep emits Jira "B Blocks A" (swap `--out`/`--in`),
  while `blocks` is unchanged. Proof: unit test on the applier for both relations.
- [ ] `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_link_sync.py` gains a `depends_on` (swap=True) case (currently
  only the `blocks`/swap=False path is tested — the gap that let this ship).
- [ ] After the fix, an inbound pass over an A depends_on B pair applies idempotently (no cycle WARNING).
- [ ] [operator-attested] one-time repair of the already-inverted Jira links (neither diff self-corrects them).
compiled_state.title: Outbound Jira link sync inverts depends_on direction (drops swap_endpoints) — backwards Jira links + perpetual cycle-warning noise
